content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statement of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662 322-5660 322-5901 322-6441 322-6444

May 19, 1983


Pauline Rusher
1098 Johns Road
Perris, CA 92370

Re: Advice No. A-83-120

Dear Ms. Rusher:

Thank you for your request for advice on the conflict of
interests provisions of the Political Reform Act. Because the
actions in question have already occurred, I can only provide
you with a general explanation of the applicable provisions of
the Act. The facts, as stated in your letter, are that you are
a City Councilmember for the City of Perris. The City
contracted with Riverside County for fire protection. A group
opposing this agreement claims that you had a conflict of
interest when you participated in the decision on the
agreement. Their allegation is based on the fact that you
worked for the California Department of Forestry, in their
Volunteers in Prevention Program, and you were paid $127.20, in
1981, and $24.35, in 1982.

Government Code Section 87100l/ prohibits a public
official from making, or participating in the making of a
governmental decision in which she knows or has reason to know
she has a financial interest. An official has a "financial
interest" in a decision within the meaning of Section 87100 if
it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will materially
affect a source of income aggregating $250 or more received by
the public official within 12 months prior to the time when the
decision is made. (Section 87103(c).)

Thus, if a source of income did not provide an official with
at least $250 in income within the last 12 months, the official

_________________
1/ Hereinafter all statutory references are to the
Government Code, unless otherwise indicated.

Pauline Rusher
May 19, 1983
Page 2


nas no need to disqualify herself on decisions affecting that
source.

In addition, salary received from a governmental agency is
excluded from tne Act's definition of "income." Thus, if
payments qualify as "salary," the governmental agency is not
considered to be a source of income, regardless of the amount of
payments provided to the official.

If you have any additional questions, please feel free to
contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Very truly yours,

Janis Shank McLean
Janis Shank McLean
Counsel
Legal Division

JSM:pln

Mar 3 8 22 AM '83

Barbara Palmer
Fair Oaks Obedience Commission
P.O. Box 807
Lafayette, Ca 95804

Dear Ms. Palmer;

I am a City Councilman in the City of Prise and
the City has executive agreement with Roseville
County for fire protection. The agreement has kept
us from to stop the agreement. They have stated
that I have a conflict of interest because I
worked for the California Department of Investigation
their U.S.( mountain in Invention) program. My
1982 W-2 shows I earned $34.55 and in
1981 $129.20

I await the new financial if you would
send a letter stating weather or not I
able is conflict in quite in lighter
of the law that private is the situation,

Thank you,

Patrice E. Lekel

[Address at top right:]
Patrice E. Lekel
1098 Spice Road
Prise, Calif. 93370

MAR 3 [date stamp]

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662 322-5660 322-5901 322-6441 322-6444

May 3, 1983

Councilwoman Pauline L. Rusher
1098 Johns Road
Perris, CA 92370

Re: A-83-120

Dear Councilwoman Rusher:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political
Reform Act has been referred to Janis Shank McLaren, an
attorney in the Legal Division of the Fair Political
Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact this attorney directly at
(916) 322-5901.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly.
Therefore, unless your request poses particularly complex
legal questions, or unless more information is needed to
answer your request, you should expect a response within 21
working days.

Very truly yours,

Barbara A. Millman
Chief, Legal Division

BAM:plh
Enclosure

sections.question_synthetic: Whether a City Councilmember has a conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act when participating in a decision regarding a city fire protection contract, given that the councilmember previously received minimal compensation ($127.20 in 1981 and $24.55 in 1982) from the California Department of Forestry's Volunteers in Prevention Program.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: A public official has a financial interest requiring disqualification only if a decision will materially affect a source of income aggregating $250 or more within the preceding 12 months. Additionally, salary from governmental agencies is excluded from the Act's definition of income. The councilmember's minimal payments from the Department of Forestry do not meet the $250 threshold and likely qualify as salary, thus creating no conflict of interest.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains no explicit QUESTION or CONCLUSION section headers, but letter structure clearly presents the legal question and FPPC's analytical response. Advice is responsive to the requestor's factual scenario. Minor OCR artifacts present but do not impede meaning.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a City Councilmember has a conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act when participating in a decision regarding a city fire protection contract, given that the councilmember previously received minimal compensation ($127.20 in 1981 and $24.55 in 1982) from the California Department of Forestry's Volunteers in Prevention Program.

CONCLUSION: A public official has a financial interest requiring disqualification only if a decision will materially affect a source of income aggregating $250 or more within the preceding 12 months. Additionally, salary from governmental agencies is excluded from the Act's definition of income. The councilmember's minimal payments from the Department of Forestry do not meet the $250 threshold and likely qualify as salary, thus creating no conflict of interest.

embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter responding to Pauline Rusher, a City Councilmember for Perris, regarding allegations of conflict of interest in a city decision on a fire protection contract. The FPPC explains that no conflict exists because the councilmember's payments from the state Department of Forestry fell below the $250 threshold and likely qualify as excludable governmental salary.
